summary: Reconstructed major parts of src/melee/if/ifprize.c, replacing placeholder calls with real prize-interface state-machine, setup, text, audio, and cleanup logic. The decomp.dev report recorded +1116 matched bytes: fn_802FE470 and un_802FE6A8 became 100% matches, while un_802FE918 and un_802FEBE0_OnEnter improved to near-match status; the only noted downside was a .data regression.

key_files.role: Only changed file; refined ifprize global state and reconstructed the setup, update callback, display text/audio, and OnEnter initialization paths.

what_changed: Added placeholder.h include, used by the file for stack-padding helpers such as PAD_STACK.
Moved and expanded struct un_802FEBE0_OnEnter_arg0 to include x0, x2, x4, and a self-referential x8 pointer, matching the linked-list style traversal used by fn_802FE470.

Refined global state struct un_803F9D48 from padding/void/int fields into typed offsets: HSD_JObj, HSD_GObj, HSD_Text pointers, linked arg pointers, unsigned-short text/id fields, and x30[3] initialized to 0x43, 0x44, 0x45.

Replaced placeholder body of fn_802FE470 with a state machine over un_803F9D48.x1: delayed initial display, input handling via gm_801A36A0(4) & 0x1100, linked prize advancement, transition to fade/exit, GObj/archive cleanup, and JObj/DObj animation requests.

Completed un_802FE6A8 setup logic for camera, light, and UI GObjs from SceneDesc data; stored created GObjs/JObj/Text handles in un_803F9D48 for later update and teardown; configured SisLib text fitting, alignment, kerning, and color.

Replaced un_802FE918 placeholder calls with display logic: audio trigger selection from un_803F9D48.x30, random next sound index, prize-table lookup through un_803F9B30/un_803F9A00, special handling for a == 0x3E, SisLib text updates, date/time retrieval, US vs non-US sprintf formatting, and positioned date text.

Improved un_802FEBE0_OnEnter initialization by storing arg0/x2C pointers, choosing an initial random x3, setting state flags/fields, changing arg0x0 typing/order, and routing the un_802FE3F8 call through a wrapper intended to avoid unwanted inlining.
smart_moves: Turning un_803F9D48's anonymous padding into a typed scene-state struct made later functions straightforward: setup stores camera/light/UI/text handles, the callback animates and tears them down, and text rendering reuses the same HSD_Text pointers.
The arg struct was modeled as a self-linked list, which explains why fn_802FE470 can advance un_803F9D48.x2C after each input-triggered prize display.

The old broad #pragma dont_inline around un_802FE3F8 was replaced with a targeted two-layer inline wrapper, un_802FE3F8_inner and un_802FE3F8_noinline, documented with the same inlining constraint for un_802FEBE0_OnEnter.
Stack-sensitive setup code in un_802FE6A8 was isolated in an inner block with GXColor and PAD_STACK(0x14), preserving local lifetime/order while still letting text handles live in the global state.

Matching details were handled with narrow casts and exact temporaries: HSD_GObj_804D784B/A masked with & 0xFF, animation frame cast through (s8) before f32, text id fields passed through short pointers, and a local unsigned-short v_x8 in the special text path.
The callback uses an early return after state-3 cleanup, avoiding the animation-update tail for destroyed objects and matching the logical teardown boundary.
decomp_lessons: For UI/state files, replace padding with typed state fields as soon as cross-function usage proves the layout; persistent HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, and HSD_Text handles often explain update and cleanup functions.
Self-referential argument structs can reveal screen queues or chained entries; watch for arg->x8 patterns before treating tail fields as plain ints.
MWCC inlining problems do not always require file-wide pragmas; small wrapper layers can influence one call site while leaving the real helper normal.
Stack layout in setup functions may depend on scoping locals and explicit PAD_STACK sizes, especially around GXColor and text-construction calls.
When matching animation/text code, signedness and width are important: unsigned-short state fields, short* casts, and (s8)-to-f32 casts can be the difference between near-match and match.
Bot metrics can hide tradeoffs: this PR gained large code matches but regressed .data by 31 bytes, so struct initializers and data layout should be revisited after function work lands.
naming_conventions: Unknown symbols kept address-based names such as fn_802FE470, un_802FE918, un_802FEBE0_OnEnter, and un_803F9D48.
The temporary arg struct was named after the known consumer, un_802FEBE0_OnEnter_arg0, which is useful before semantic names are known.
Global state fields follow x-offset naming, with arrays such as x30[3] for contiguous data.
Known library/object types were used in the struct even while field names stayed unknown: HSD_JObj*, HSD_GObj*, HSD_Text*, HSD_Archive*, and SceneDesc*.
Local names are role-based where evidence is clear, such as gobj_camera, gobj_light, gobj_ui, jobj_ui, color, new_x3, sp1C, and sp14.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used un_802FE3F8_inner plus un_802FE3F8_noinline wrapper to prevent or redirect unwanted inlining into un_802FEBE0_OnEnter.
Used PAD_STACK(0x14) inside a nested block in un_802FE6A8 after moving GXColor into that block.
Masked HSD_GObj_804D784B and HSD_GObj_804D784A with & 0xFF before HSD_GObjObject_80390A70 calls.
Used (f32)(s8) un_803F9D48.x2 for HSD_JObjReqAnimAll while using (f32) un_803F9D48.x4 for DObj animation calls.
Passed unsigned-short state fields through (short*) casts to un_802FE3F8 and stored x8 in a local unsigned short before SisLib calls.
Used goto found and explicit i = 0 fallback in table lookup loops over un_803F9B30.
Changed OnEnter local ordering/typing by making arg0x0 an int and assigning i = 0 before reading arg0->x0.
Preserved date-format branches with exact sprintf format strings for lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS versus non-US layouts.
review_feedback: No human PR body or review comments were present in the slice.
decomp-dev[bot] reported GALE01 matched code at 69.20%, +0.03%, +1116 bytes.
Bot listed 2 new matches: fn_802FE470 +459 bytes, 19.28% to 100.00%; un_802FE6A8 +95 bytes, 82.77% to 100.00%.
Bot listed 2 improved unmatched items: un_802FE918 +542 bytes, 23.11% to 99.23%; un_802FEBE0_OnEnter +15 bytes, 95.00% to 99.93%.
Bot noted 1 regression: main/melee/if/ifprize .data -31 bytes, 96.59% to 93.59%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching ifprize .data or un_803F9D48 initializer layout after PR 2538.
Search for future semantic renames of fn_802FE470, un_802FE918, un_802FE6A8, and un_802FEBE0_OnEnter.
Look for other PRs using wrapper layers to avoid MWCC inlining instead of #pragma dont_inline.
Compare HSD_SisLib date/time formatting patterns in other interface screens.
Investigate the real semantics of un_802FEBE0_OnEnter_arg0 fields x0, x2, x4, and linked x8.
